title: EU grants €2.7 billion to clean industry projects
content: Some 54 clean industry projects will receive €2.7 billion of funding from the EU’s Innovation Fund. The projects span 17 countries and will support innovative, decarbonisation solutions such as refineries, cement and lime, renewable energy, as well as maritime, aviation and road transport.

title: Remembering the Brussels terror attacks 10 years later
content: This week marks 10 years since the terrorist attacks in Brussels in 2016. As commemorations take place to remember the victims and survivors, we take look at what the EU is doing to fight terrorism and extremism and to boost security.

title: EU Inc.:making business easier in the European Union
content: The European Commission has presented EU Inc., a new single set of corporate rules that will be the same and apply across the EU. This will make it easier for businesses to start, operate and grow across the EU. Its optional, fully digital procedures will enable innovative companies to scale up.

title: EU to modernise how it tackles rising wildfire threat
content: The Commission has put forward a new strategy for wildfire risk management that covers prevention, preparedness, response and recovery. 2025 was the worst wildfire season in Europe since records began. This strategy will help enhance resilience to wildfires and protect people and the environment.

title: The EU in 2025: a year of clarity and resolve
content: In a year where the world became more volatile and fragmented, the EU assumed more responsibility for its own security and stepped up efforts to boost its defence. It also continued to deliver for citizens and businesses. Find out more from the General report on the activities of the EU in 2025.

title: New strategy embeds gender equality into every aspect of life
content: Ahead of International Women's Day on 8 March, the European Commission has presented its gender equality strategy for 2026-2030. It sets out concrete actions to empower women and girls, engage men and boys, oppose any backtracking on fundamental rights, and accelerate progress.

title: EU releases first data on minimum wages in 2026
content: According to the first EU data on minimum wages for 2026, minimum wages in the EU ranged on 1 January from €620 per month in Bulgaria to €2 704 in Luxembourg. Find out more about why the minimum wage is key and why wages don’t tell the full story when it comes to local spending power.

title: EU takes aim at trafficking of illicit firearms
content: Illicit firearms are a threat to public safety, fuelling terrorism, organised crime, and other offences. The Commission has proposed new EU-wide rules to tackle trafficking and other firearms-related offences. These will make investigations and prosecutions more effective and help law enforcement.

title: Four years on: Europe stands with Ukraine
content: On February 24 2022, Russia launched an unprovoked war of aggression against Ukraine. Since then Ukrainians have endured years of unnecessary suffering. The EU has provided unwavering political, financial, military and humanitarian aid since the outset and reaffirms its support to Ukraine today.

title: European Citizens’ Initiative – 15 years and going strong!
content: This year we celebrate 15 years of the European Citizens’ Initiative - a unique way for you to act on causes you care about. Launch an initiative that falls within the EU’s competences and shape the EU by calling on the European Commission to propose new laws on your chosen issue. Find out more.

title: EU project works with fishers to reduce marine bycatch
content: Every year, thousands of marine animals and seabirds are accidentally caught in fishing gear across European waters. An EU project is working with fishers to test practical solutions that reduce bycatch while maintaining viable fisheries in the Atlantic, Baltic and Mediterranean seas. Find out more.

title: Horizon Europe transport projects showcase promising results at conference
content: The 2026 Results from Road Transport Research Conference showcased the results from almost 100 Horizon Europe transport projects. These demonstrated advances in decarbonisation, automated mobility, digitalisation, road safety, electric vehicles, urban mobility, infrastructure and logistics.
